todaysEvents.summary: Kuwait confirmed full output suspension (Mar 10 AM) as onshore storage reaches capacity — GCC supply deficit confirmed at 5.2M bpd. IRGC Coast Guard seized Liberian-flagged VLCC Pacific Gallant in Gulf of Oman; 28 crew held. Hormuz AIS transits at ~5/day (96% below 138/day historical average). UN Security Council veto deadlock: Russia/China vetoed ceasefire draft; US vetoed Iranian counter-resolution; ASEAN abstained. (Mar 10 AM)
todaysEvents.delta: Kuwait halted; VLCC seized; UN deadlock

todaysEvents.summary: Trump de-escalation signal (Mar 10 PM): President Trump said the US is 'considering taking over the Strait of Hormuz' and indicated the war 'may end soon.' Brent crude crashed -17.7% intraday from $109.80 to $90.35/bbl — largest single-day oil price drop since the COVID demand collapse in 2020. Markets interpreted signal as opening a bilateral back-channel; no ceasefire confirmed. Kuwait halt and Hormuz closure structurally intact. (Mar 10 PM)

ASEAN markets reversed sharply on Trump signal: PHP strengthened to 59.17 (-0.6%), IDR to 16,745 (-2.2%), THB to 31.18 (-3.2%); MYR weakened to 4.0440 (+2.0%) on reduced oil windfall. iTraxx Asia IG tightened -16bp to 152bp; ASEAN HY -28bp to 480bp. Regional Shipping index +6.0%; ASEAN Energy Equities -8.6%. Signal volatility remains high — military posture unchanged. (Mar 10 close)

scenarios.label: Base
scenarios.description: Trump de-escalation signal (Mar 10 PM) opens bilateral back-channel pathway — probability upgraded from 18% as markets price in negotiated off-ramp within 2–4 weeks. Hormuz disruption lasts 2–4 weeks total; Kuwait reinstates output as storage clears. Brent stabilises at $85–100/bbl. Saudi/UAE spare capacity (3-week ramp) partially offsets Iraq/Kuwait supply deficit after Hormuz reopens. ASEAN LNG buyers source Australian and US spot cargoes. P&I clubs restore cover upon ceasefire confirmation. Goldman Q4 2026 normalisation forecast at $60/bbl contingent on swift resolution. Risk: Trump signals volatile and military posture unchanged.

scenarios.label: Stress
scenarios.description: Hormuz closure extends 1–3 months; Indian Ocean front expands with further IRGC naval actions. VLCC Pacific Gallant seizure signals deliberate escalation beyond Gulf. P&I blackout durable; 329 trapped tankers cleared only partially. GCC supply deficit confirmed at 5.2M bpd; Kuwait output fully halted; Iraq output -60%. Brent sustains $110–130/bbl. JKM LNG re-spikes to $22–25/MMBtu as Qatar restart timeline extends. ASEAN current account deficits widen sharply; Bank Indonesia and BSP forced into emergency interventions. Indonesian subsidy blowout accelerates.

scenarios.label: Tail
scenarios.description: Full Hormuz closure sustained beyond 3 months; IRGC acts on Malacca threat following VLCC seizure precedent; Indian Ocean naval escalation draws in additional parties. Oil at $150–200/bbl (J.P. Morgan upper bound). LNG effectively unavailable for spot buyers; Qatar's 77 mtpa full restart blocked. Iraq, Kuwait, UAE combined output loss exceeds 6M bpd — global supply deficit unprecedented since 1973. Recession probability >70% for Thailand and Philippines. ASEAN EM bonds face mass outflows; P&I cover does not return for 6+ months; global trade finance seizes.
